title: teucrium dunense subsp. sublittoralis
intro: Teucrium dunense subsp. sublittoralis is a subspecies of the Teucrium dunense plant, commonly known as the "Mediterranean germander" or "Dunedin tea tree", which is a type of shrubby perennial plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving cognitive function, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the leaves of the plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
